1→"""Flask web application serving the DevTools Scrape frontend and JSON API."""
     2→
     3→import os
     4→import secrets
     5→import time
     6→import uuid
     7→from collections import defaultdict
     8→from datetime import datetime
     9→from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
    10→
    11→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    12→from flask import Flask, render_template, request, jsonify, g
    13→
    14→from database import (
    15→    SOURCE_REGISTRY,
    16→    classify_source,
    17→    count_all_startups,
    18→    count_search_results,
    19→    count_startups_by_source_key,
    20→    get_all_startups,
    21→    get_last_scrape_time,
    22→    get_related_startups,
    23→    get_source_counts,
    24→    get_startup_by_id,
    25→    get_startups_by_source_key,
    26→    init_db,
    27→    search_startups,
    28→)
    29→from chatbot import generate_chat_response
    30→from logging_config import bind_context, get_logger, unbind_context
    31→
    32→# Load environment variables
    33→load_dotenv()
    34→
    35→app = Flask(__name__)
    36→logger = get_logger("devtools.app")
    37→
    38→# Initialize database
    39→init_db()
    40→
    41→# Production configuration
    42→_secret_key = os.getenv('SECRET_KEY')
    43→if not _secret_key:
    44→    _secret_key = secrets.token_hex(32)
    45→    logger.warning("config.generated_secret_key", extra={"event": "config.generated_secret_key"})
    46→app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = _secret_key
    47→app.config['DEBUG'] = False
    48→
    49→def _truthy_env(var_name: str, default: bool = False) -> bool:
    50→    """Parse an environment variable as a boolean flag."""
    51→    value = os.getenv(var_name)
    52→    if value is None:
    53→        return default
    54→    return value.lower() in {"1", "true", "yes", "on"}
    55→
    56→
    57→def _safe_float_env(var_name: str, default: float) -> float:
    58→    """Read an environment variable as a float, returning default on failure."""
    59→    raw_value = os.getenv(var_name)
    60→    if raw_value is None:
    61→        return default
    62→    try:
    63→        return float(raw_value)
    64→    except (TypeError, ValueError):
    65→        return default
    66→
    67→
    68→def _parse_csv_env(var_name: str) -> list[str]:
    69→    """Split a comma-separated environment variable into a list of strings."""
    70→    raw_value = os.getenv(var_name, "")
    71→    return [item.strip() for item in raw_value.split(",") if item.strip()]
    72→
    73→
    74→def _safe_int(value, default: int) -> int:
    75→    """Convert value to int, returning default on failure."""
    76→    try:
    77→        return int(value)
    78→    except (TypeError, ValueError):
    79→        return default
    80→
    81→
    82→def _rum_script_source(site: str) -> str:
    83→    """Return the Datadog RUM browser agent script URL for the given site."""
    84→    region_map = {
    85→        "datadoghq.com": "us1",
    86→        "datadoghq.eu": "eu1",
    87→        "us3.datadoghq.com": "us3",
    88→        "us5.datadoghq.com": "us5",
    89→        "ap1.datadoghq.com": "ap1",
    90→        "ddog-gov.com": "us1-gov",
    91→    }
    92→    region = region_map.get(site.lower(), "us1")
    93→    version = os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_BROWSER_VERSION", "v6")
    94→    version_segment = version if version.startswith("v") else f"v{version}"
    95→    return f"https://www.datadoghq-browser-agent.com/{region}/{version_segment}/datadog-rum.js"
    96→
    97→
    98→def _build_rum_context() -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    99→    """Build the Datadog RUM configuration dict, or None when credentials are missing."""
   100→    application_id = os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_APPLICATION_ID")
   101→    client_token = os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_CLIENT_TOKEN")
   102→    if not application_id or not client_token:
   103→        return None
   104→
   105→    site = os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_SITE", os.getenv("DD_SITE", "datadoghq.com"))
   106→    service_name = os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_SERVICE", os.getenv("DD_SERVICE", "devtoolscrape"))
   107→    environment = os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_ENV", os.getenv("DD_ENV", "prod"))
   108→    version = os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_VERSION", os.getenv("DD_VERSION", "1.1"))
   109→    allowed_tracing_urls = _parse_csv_env("DATADOG_RUM_ALLOWED_TRACING_URLS")
   110→    if not allowed_tracing_urls:
   111→        allowed_tracing_urls = [
   112→            "https://devtoolscrape.com",
   113→            "https://*.devtoolscrape.com",
   114→        ]
   115→        if request:
   116→            allowed_tracing_urls.append(request.host_url.rstrip("/"))
   117→
   118→    rum_config = {
   119→        "applicationId": application_id,
   120→        "clientToken": client_token,
   121→        "site": site,
   122→        "service": service_name,
   123→        "env": environment,
   124→        "version": version,
   125→        "sampleRate": _safe_float_env("DATADOG_RUM_SAMPLE_RATE", 100.0),
   126→        "traceSampleRate": _safe_float_env("DATADOG_RUM_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE", 100.0),
   127→        "profilingSampleRate": _safe_float_env("DATADOG_RUM_PROFILING_SAMPLE_RATE", 100.0),
   128→        "tracePropagationMode": os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_TRACE_PROPAGATION_MODE", "datadog"),
   129→        "trackUserInteractions": _truthy_env("DATADOG_RUM_TRACK_USER_INTERACTIONS", True),
   130→        "trackResources": _truthy_env("DATADOG_RUM_TRACK_RESOURCES", True),
   131→        "trackLongTasks": _truthy_env("DATADOG_RUM_TRACK_LONG_TASKS", True),
   132→        "defaultPrivacyLevel": os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_DEFAULT_PRIVACY_LEVEL", "allow"),
   133→        "allowedTracingUrls": allowed_tracing_urls,
   134→    }
   135→
   136→    session_replay_enabled = _truthy_env("DATADOG_RUM_SESSION_REPLAY", False)
   137→    if session_replay_enabled:
   138→        rum_config["sessionReplaySampleRate"] = _safe_float_env(
   139→            "DATADOG_RUM_SESSION_REPLAY_SAMPLE_RATE",
   140→            _safe_float_env("DATADOG_RUM_SAMPLE_RATE", 100.0),
   141→        )
   142→
   143→    return {
   144→        "config": rum_config,
   145→        "script_src": os.getenv("DATADOG_RUM_SCRIPT_SRC", _rum_script_source(site)),
   146→        "enable_session_replay": session_replay_enabled,
   147→    }
   148→
   149→
   150→@app.context_processor
   151→def inject_datadog_rum() -> Dict[str, Any]:
   152→    """Inject Datadog RUM configuration into every template context."""
   153→    return {"datadog_rum": _build_rum_context()}
   154→
   155→if not app.debug:
   156→    from werkzeug.middleware.proxy_fix import ProxyFix
   157→    app.wsgi_app = ProxyFix(app.wsgi_app, x_proto=1, x_host=1)
   158→
   159→@app.before_request
   160→def _start_request_logging():
   161→    request_id = request.headers.get("X-Request-ID") or str(uuid.uuid4())
   162→    g.request_id = request_id
   163→    g.request_start_time = time.perf_counter()
   164→    client_ip = request.headers.get("X-Forwarded-For", request.remote_addr)
   165→    bind_context(
   166→        request_id=request_id,
   167→        http_method=request.method,
   168→        http_path=request.path,
   169→        client_ip=client_ip,
   170→    )
   171→    logger.debug(
   172→        "request.start",
   173→        extra={
   174→            "event": "request.start",
   175→            "http_method": request.method,
   176→            "http_path": request.path,
   177→            "client_ip": client_ip,
   178→        },
   179→    )
   180→
   181→
   182→@app.after_request
   183→def _complete_request_logging(response):
   184→    duration_ms = None
   185→    if hasattr(g, "request_start_time"):
   186→        duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - g.request_start_time) * 1000, 2)
   187→    logger.info(
   188→        "request.complete",
   189→        extra={
   190→            "event": "request.complete",
   191→            "status_code": response.status_code,
   192→            "duration_ms": duration_ms,
   193→            "content_length": response.calculate_content_length(),
   194→        },
   195→    )
   196→    return response
   197→
   198→
   199→@app.teardown_request
   200→def _teardown_request_logging(exc):
   201→    if exc is not None:
   202→        logger.exception(
   203→            "request.error",
   204→            extra={
   205→                "event": "request.error",
   206→                "error_type": exc.__class__.__name__,
   207→            },
   208→        )
   209→    unbind_context("request_id", "http_method", "http_path", "client_ip")
   210→
   211→def _parse_pagination(default_per_page: int = 20, max_per_page: int = 100):
   212→    """Parse page and per_page from request args, returning (page, per_page, offset)."""
   213→    per_page = min(max(_safe_int(request.args.get('per_page', default_per_page), default_per_page), 1), max_per_page)
   214→    page = max(_safe_int(request.args.get('page', 1), 1), 1)
   215→    offset = (page - 1) * per_page
   216→    return page, per_page, offset
   217→
   218→
   219→def _total_pages(total_results: int, per_page: int) -> int:
   220→    """Compute total number of pages, minimum 1."""
   221→    return max((total_results + per_page - 1) // per_page, 1)
   222→
   223→
   224→def _pagination_vars(items: list, total_results: int, page: int, per_page: int, offset: int) -> dict:
   225→    """Compute the common pagination template variables."""
   226→    return {
   227→        "page": page,
   228→        "per_page": per_page,
   229→        "total_pages": _total_pages(total_results, per_page) if total_results else 1,
   230→        "total_results": total_results,
   231→        "first_item": offset + 1 if items else 0,
   232→        "last_item": offset + len(items),
   233→    }
   234→
   235→
   236→def summarize_sources(startups: list[Dict[str, Any]]) -> Dict[str, int]:
   237→    """Aggregate startup counts by source category."""
   238→    logger.debug(
   239→        "summarize.sources",
   240→        extra={"event": "summarize.sources", "startup_count": len(startups)},
   241→    )
   242→    counts: dict[str, int] = {"total": len(startups), "other": 0}
   243→    for key in SOURCE_REGISTRY:
   244→        counts[key] = 0
   245→
   246→    for startup in startups:
   247→        bucket = classify_source(startup["source"])
   248→        counts[bucket] += 1
   249→
   250→    return counts
   251→
   252→@app.route('/')
   253→def index():
   254→    """Main page showing all devtools"""
   255→    source_filter = request.args.get('source', '')
   256→    page, per_page, offset = _parse_pagination()
   257→
   258→    if source_filter:
   259→        total_results = count_startups_by_source_key(source_filter)
   260→        startups = get_startups_by_source_key(source_filter, limit=per_page, offset=offset)
   261→    else:
   262→        total_results = count_all_startups()
   263→        startups = get_all_startups(limit=per_page, offset=offset)
   264→
   265→    paging = _pagination_vars(startups, total_results, page, per_page, offset)
   266→    response = render_template(
   267→        'index.html',
   268→        startups=startups,
   269→        source_counts=get_source_counts(),
   270→        current_filter=source_filter,
   271→        last_scrape_time=get_last_scrape_time(),
   272→        **paging,
   273→    )
   274→    logger.info(
   275→        "render.index",
   276→        extra={
   277→            "event": "render.index",
   278→            "source_filter": source_filter or "all",
   279→            "page": page,
   280→            "per_page": per_page,
   281→            "returned": len(startups),
   282→            "total_results": total_results,
   283→        },
   284→    )
   285→    return response
   286→
   287→@app.route('/source/<source_name>')
   288→def filter_by_source(source_name):
   289→    """Filter tools by source"""
   290→    page, per_page, offset = _parse_pagination()
   291→
   292→    filtered_startups = get_startups_by_source_key(source_name, limit=per_page, offset=offset)
   293→    entry = SOURCE_REGISTRY.get(source_name)
   294→    source_display = entry["display"] if entry else "All Sources"
   295→
   296→    total_results = count_startups_by_source_key(source_name)
   297→    paging = _pagination_vars(filtered_startups, total_results, page, per_page, offset)
   298→    response = render_template(
   299→        'index.html',
   300→        startups=filtered_startups,
   301→        source_counts=get_source_counts(),
   302→        current_filter=source_name,
   303→        source_display=source_display,
   304→        last_scrape_time=get_last_scrape_time(),
   305→        **paging,
   306→    )
   307→    logger.info(
   308→        "render.source",
   309→        extra={
   310→            "event": "render.source",
   311→            "source": source_name,
   312→            "page": page,
   313→            "per_page": per_page,
   314→            "returned": len(filtered_startups),
   315→            "total_results": total_results,
   316→        },
   317→    )
   318→    return response
   319→
   320→@app.route('/search')
   321→def search():
   322→    """Search page"""
   323→    query = request.args.get('q', '')
   324→    page, per_page, offset = _parse_pagination()
   325→
   326→    if query:
   327→        total_results = count_search_results(query)
   328→        startups = search_startups(query, limit=per_page, offset=offset)
   329→    else:
   330→        total_results = 0
   331→        startups = []
   332→    paging = _pagination_vars(startups, total_results, page, per_page, offset)
   333→    response = render_template(
   334→        'search.html',
   335→        startups=startups,
   336→        query=query,
   337→        source_counts=summarize_sources(startups),
   338→        last_scrape_time=get_last_scrape_time(),
   339→        **paging,
   340→    )
   341→    logger.info(
   342→        "render.search",
   343→        extra={
   344→            "event": "render.search",
   345→            "query": query,
   346→            "page": page,
   347→            "per_page": per_page,
   348→            "returned": len(startups),
   349→            "total_results": total_results,
   350→        },
   351→    )
   352→    return response
   353→
   354→@app.route('/tool/<int:tool_id>')
   355→def tool_detail(tool_id):
   356→    """Show detailed view of a specific tool"""
   357→    tool = get_startup_by_id(tool_id)
   358→    if not tool:
   359→        logger.warning(
   360→            "render.tool_missing",
   361→            extra={"event": "render.tool_missing", "tool_id": tool_id},
   362→        )
   363→        return "Tool not found", 404
   364→
   365→    related = get_related_startups(tool['source'], tool['id'], limit=4)
   366→
   367→    last_scrape_time = get_last_scrape_time()
   368→    logger.info(
   369→        "render.tool_detail",
   370→        extra={
   371→            "event": "render.tool_detail",
   372→            "tool_id": tool_id,
   373→            "source": tool.get("source"),
   374→            "related_count": len(related),
   375→        },
   376→    )
   377→    return render_template('tool_detail.html', tool=tool, startups=related, last_scrape_time=last_scrape_time)
   378→
   379→@app.route('/api/startups')
   380→def api_startups():
   381→    """API endpoint for getting all startups"""
   382→    page, per_page, offset = _parse_pagination(default_per_page=50, max_per_page=200)
   383→
   384→    startups = get_all_startups(limit=per_page, offset=offset)
   385→    total = count_all_startups()
   386→    payload = {
   387→        'items': startups,
   388→        'total': total,
   389→        **_pagination_vars(startups, total, page, per_page, offset),
   390→    }
   391→    logger.info(
   392→        "api.startups",
   393→        extra={
   394→            "event": "api.startups",
   395→            "page": page,
   396→            "per_page": per_page,
   397→            "returned": len(startups),
   398→            "total": total,
   399→        },
   400→    )
   401→    return jsonify(payload)
   402→
   403→@app.route('/api/search')
   404→def api_search():
   405→    """API endpoint for searching startups"""
   406→    query = request.args.get('q', '')
   407→    page, per_page, offset = _parse_pagination(max_per_page=200)
   408→
   409→    if query:
   410→        total = count_search_results(query)
   411→        startups = search_startups(query, limit=per_page, offset=offset)
   412→    else:
   413→        total = 0
   414→        startups = []
   415→
   416→    payload = {
   417→        'items': startups,
   418→        'total': total,
   419→        **_pagination_vars(startups, total, page, per_page, offset),
   420→    }
   421→    logger.info(
   422→        "api.search",
   423→        extra={
   424→            "event": "api.search",
   425→            "query": query,
   426→            "page": page,
   427→            "per_page": per_page,
   428→            "returned": len(startups),
   429→            "total": total,
   430→        },
   431→    )
   432→    return jsonify(payload)
   433→
   434→
   435→# Chat endpoint rate limiting
   436→_chat_rate_limits: dict[str, list[float]] = defaultdict(list)
   437→_CHAT_RATE_LIMIT = int(os.getenv("CHATBOT_RATE_LIMIT", "10"))
   438→_CHAT_RATE_WINDOW = 60
   439→
   440→
   441→@app.route('/api/chat', methods=['POST'])
   442→def api_chat():
   443→    """API endpoint for chatbot tool recommendations."""
   444→    client_ip = request.headers.get("X-Forwarded-For", request.remote_addr)
   445→    now = time.time()
   446→    _chat_rate_limits[client_ip] = [
   447→        t for t in _chat_rate_limits[client_ip] if now - t < _CHAT_RATE_WINDOW
   448→    ]
   449→    if len(_chat_rate_limits[client_ip]) >= _CHAT_RATE_LIMIT:
   450→        logger.warning(
   451→            "api.chat.rate_limited",
   452→            extra={"event": "api.chat.rate_limited", "client_ip": client_ip},
   453→        )
   454→        return jsonify({
   455→            'error': 'Rate limit exceeded',
   456→            'response': 'You are sending messages too quickly. Please wait a moment and try again.',
   457→            'tools': [],
   458→        }), 429
   459→    _chat_rate_limits[client_ip].append(now)
   460→
   461→    data = request.get_json(silent=True)
   462→    user_message = (data.get("message", "") if data else "").strip()
   463→    if not user_message:
   464→        return jsonify({"error": "Message is required"}), 400
   465→    if len(user_message) > 500:
   466→        return jsonify({"error": "Message too long (max 500 characters)"}), 400
   467→
   468→    result = generate_chat_response(user_message)
   469→    logger.info(
   470→        "api.chat",
   471→        extra={
   472→            "event": "api.chat",
   473→            "message_length": len(user_message),
   474→            "tools_found": len(result.get("tools", [])),
   475→        },
   476→    )
   477→    return jsonify(result)
   478→
   479→
   480→@app.route('/health')
   481→def health_check():
   482→    """Health check endpoint for monitoring"""
   483→    logger.debug("health_check", extra={"event": "health_check"})
   484→    return jsonify({
   485→        'status': 'healthy',
   486→        'timestamp': datetime.now().isoformat(),
   487→        'database': 'connected'
   488→    })
   489→
   490→
   491→@app.template_filter('format_date')
   492→def format_date(date_str):
   493→    """Format date for display"""
   494→    if isinstance(date_str, str):
   495→        try:
   496→            dt = datetime.fromisoformat(date_str.replace('Z', '+00:00'))
   497→            return dt.strftime('%B %d, %Y')
   498→        except (ValueError, TypeError):
   499→            return date_str
   500→    return date_str
   501→
   502→
   503→@app.template_filter('format_datetime')
   504→def format_datetime(date_str):
   505→    """Format datetime for display"""
   506→    if isinstance(date_str, str):
   507→        try:
   508→            dt = datetime.fromisoformat(date_str.replace('Z', '+00:00'))
   509→            return dt.strftime('%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p')
   510→        except (ValueError, TypeError):
   511→            return date_str
   512→    return date_str
   513→
   514→
   515→# Error handlers
   516→@app.errorhandler(404)
   517→def not_found_error(error):
   518→    logger.warning("http.404", extra={"event": "http.404"})
   519→    return render_template('404.html'), 404
   520→
   521→
   522→@app.errorhandler(500)
   523→def internal_error(error):
   524→    logger.exception("http.500", extra={"event": "http.500"})
   525→    return render_template('500.html'), 500
   526→
   527→if __name__ == '__main__':
   528→    # Production settings
   529→    port = int(os.getenv('PORT', 8000))
   530→    host = os.getenv('HOST', '0.0.0.0')
   531→    
   532→    app.run(host=host, port=port, debug=False)
   533→

1→"""SQLite persistence layer with FTS5 full-text search for developer tools."""
     2→
     3→import os
     4→import sqlite3
     5→import time
     6→from contextlib import contextmanager
     7→from datetime import datetime
     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, Iterator, Optional
    10→
    11→from logging_config import get_logger
    12→
    13→logger = get_logger("devtools.db")
    14→
    15→
    16→SOURCE_REGISTRY: dict[str, dict] = {
    17→    "github": {
    18→        "display": "GitHub Trending",
    19→        "where": "source = ?",
    20→        "params": ["GitHub Trending"],
    21→        "match": lambda s: s == "GitHub Trending",
    22→    },
    23→    "hackernews": {
    24→        "display": "Hacker News",
    25→        "where": "source LIKE ? OR source LIKE ?",
    26→        "params": ["Hacker News%", "Show HN%"],
    27→        "match": lambda s: s.startswith("Hacker News") or s.startswith("Show HN"),
    28→    },
    29→    "producthunt": {
    30→        "display": "Product Hunt",
    31→        "where": "source = ?",
    32→        "params": ["Product Hunt"],
    33→        "match": lambda s: s == "Product Hunt",
    34→    },
    35→}
    36→
    37→
    38→def classify_source(source: str) -> str:
    39→    """Classify a source string into a registry key, or 'other'."""
    40→    for key, info in SOURCE_REGISTRY.items():
    41→        if info["match"](source):
    42→            return key
    43→    return "other"
    44→
    45→DEFAULT_DATA_DIR = Path(os.getcwd()) / "data"
    46→DATA_DIR = Path(os.getenv("DEVTOOLS_DATA_DIR", DEFAULT_DATA_DIR))
    47→DATA_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    48→
    49→DB_PATH = Path(os.getenv("DEVTOOLS_DB_PATH", DATA_DIR / "startups.db"))
    50→DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    51→DB_NAME = str(DB_PATH)
    52→
    53→
    54→def _connect() -> sqlite3.Connection:
    55→    """Create a new SQLite connection with Row factory enabled."""
    56→    start = time.perf_counter()
    57→    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
    58→    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    59→    duration = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)
    60→    logger.debug(
    61→        "db.connect",
    62→        extra={"event": "db.connect", "duration_ms": duration, "db_path": str(DB_PATH)},
    63→    )
    64→    return conn
    65→
    66→
    67→@contextmanager
    68→def _db_connection() -> Iterator[sqlite3.Connection]:
    69→    """Open a database connection and ensure it is closed after use."""
    70→    conn = _connect()
    71→    try:
    72→        yield conn
    73→    finally:
    74→        conn.close()
    75→
    76→
    77→def _row_to_dict(row: sqlite3.Row) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    78→    """Convert a sqlite3.Row to a plain dictionary."""
    79→    return dict(row)
    80→
    81→
    82→def init_db() -> None:
    83→    """Initialize the database schema, creating tables and indices if needed.
    84→
    85→    Retries once on OperationalError to handle transient filesystem issues.
    86→    """
    87→    logger.info("db.init.start", extra={"event": "db.init.start", "db_path": str(DB_PATH)})
    88→    conn = None
    89→    for attempt in range(2):
    90→        try:
    91→            conn = _connect()
    92→            break
    93→        except sqlite3.OperationalError:
    94→            if attempt == 1:
    95→                raise
    96→            continue
    97→
    98→    try:
    99→        c = conn.cursor()
   100→
   101→        c.execute('''
   102→            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS startups (
   103→                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   104→                name TEXT NOT NULL,
   105→                url TEXT UNIQUE,
   106→                description TEXT,
   107→                source TEXT,
   108→                date_found TIMESTAMP
   109→            )
   110→        ''')
   111→
   112→        # Add index on name for faster duplicate checking
   113→        c.execute('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_startups_name ON startups(name)')
   114→        c.execute('CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_startups_source ON startups(source)')
   115→
   116→        # Create table for tracking last scrape time
   117→        c.execute('''
   118→            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS scrape_log (
   119→                id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   120→                last_scrape TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
   121→                scrapers_run TEXT
   122→            )
   123→        ''')
   124→
   125→        # Create FTS index for fast search
   126→        c.execute('''
   127→            CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS startups_fts
   128→            USING fts5(name, description, content='startups', content_rowid='id')
   129→        ''')
   130→        c.executescript('''
   131→            CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS startups_ai AFTER INSERT ON startups BEGIN
   132→                INSERT INTO startups_fts(rowid, name, description) VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.description);
   133→            END;
   134→            CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS startups_ad AFTER DELETE ON startups BEGIN
   135→                INSERT INTO startups_fts(startups_fts, rowid, name, description) VALUES('delete', old.id, old.name, old.description);
   136→            END;
   137→            CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS startups_au AFTER UPDATE ON startups BEGIN
   138→                INSERT INTO startups_fts(startups_fts, rowid, name, description) VALUES('delete', old.id, old.name, old.description);
   139→                INSERT INTO startups_fts(rowid, name, description) VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.description);
   140→            END;
   141→        ''')
   142→        try:
   143→            c.execute("INSERT INTO startups_fts(startups_fts) VALUES('rebuild')")
   144→        except sqlite3.OperationalError:
   145→            # Rebuild may fail if table is empty or FTS not initialised yet; safe to ignore
   146→            pass
   147→
   148→        conn.commit()
   149→    finally:
   150→        conn.close()
   151→    logger.info("db.init.complete", extra={"event": "db.init.complete"})
   152→
   153→
   154→def is_duplicate(name: str, url: str) -> bool:
   155→    """Check if a startup already exists by name or URL."""
   156→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   157→        cursor = conn.cursor()
   158→        cursor.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM startups WHERE name = ? OR url = ?", (name, url))
   159→        count = cursor.fetchone()[0]
   160→    logger.debug(
   161→        "db.is_duplicate",
   162→        extra={
   163→            "event": "db.is_duplicate",
   164→            "startup_name": name,
   165→            "url": url,
   166→            "is_duplicate": count > 0,
   167→        },
   168→    )
   169→    return count > 0
   170→
   171→
   172→def save_startup(startup: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   173→    """Persist a startup record, skipping duplicates by name or URL."""
   174→    if is_duplicate(startup['name'], startup['url']):
   175→        logger.warning(
   176→            "db.startup_duplicate",
   177→            extra={
   178→                "event": "db.startup_duplicate",
   179→                "startup_name": startup.get('name'),
   180→                "url": startup.get('url'),
   181→                "reason": "name_or_url_match",
   182→            },
   183→        )
   184→        return
   185→
   186→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   187→        c = conn.cursor()
   188→        try:
   189→            c.execute('''
   190→                INSERT INTO startups (name, url, description, source, date_found)
   191→                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   192→            ''', (
   193→                startup['name'],
   194→                startup['url'],
   195→                startup['description'],
   196→                startup['source'],
   197→                startup['date_found']
   198→            ))
   199→            conn.commit()
   200→            logger.info(
   201→                "db.startup_saved",
   202→                extra={
   203→                    "event": "db.startup_saved",
   204→                    "startup_name": startup.get('name'),
   205→                    "url": startup.get('url'),
   206→                    "source": startup.get('source'),
   207→                },
   208→            )
   209→        except sqlite3.IntegrityError:
   210→            # URL already exists (race condition fallback)
   211→            logger.warning(
   212→                "db.startup_duplicate",
   213→                extra={
   214→                    "event": "db.startup_duplicate",
   215→                    "startup_name": startup.get('name'),
   216→                    "url": startup.get('url'),
   217→                },
   218→            )
   219→
   220→
   221→def get_startup_by_id(startup_id: int) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   222→    """Fetch a single startup by its primary key."""
   223→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   224→        row = conn.execute(
   225→            '''
   226→            SELECT id, name, url, description, source, date_found
   227→            FROM startups WHERE id = ?
   228→            ''',
   229→            (startup_id,),
   230→        ).fetchone()
   231→    result = _row_to_dict(row) if row else None
   232→    logger.debug(
   233→        "db.get_startup_by_id",
   234→        extra={
   235→            "event": "db.get_startup_by_id",
   236→            "startup_id": startup_id,
   237→            "found": bool(result),
   238→        },
   239→    )
   240→    return result
   241→
   242→
   243→def get_related_startups(source: str, exclude_id: int, limit: int = 4) -> list[Dict[str, Any]]:
   244→    """Fetch startups from the same source, excluding a given ID."""
   245→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   246→        rows = conn.execute(
   247→            '''
   248→            SELECT id, name, url, description, source, date_found
   249→            FROM startups
   250→            WHERE source = ? AND id != ?
   251→            ORDER BY date_found DESC
   252→            LIMIT ?
   253→            ''',
   254→            (source, exclude_id, limit),
   255→        ).fetchall()
   256→    results = [_row_to_dict(row) for row in rows]
   257→    logger.debug(
   258→        "db.get_related_startups",
   259→        extra={
   260→            "event": "db.get_related_startups",
   261→            "source": source,
   262→            "exclude_id": exclude_id,
   263→            "limit": limit,
   264→            "returned": len(results),
   265→        },
   266→    )
   267→    return results
   268→
   269→
   270→def get_startups_by_sources(where_clause: str, params: Iterable, limit: Optional[int] = None, offset: Optional[int] = None) -> list[Dict[str, Any]]:
   271→    """Query startups matching a dynamic WHERE clause with optional pagination."""
   272→    query = '''
   273→        SELECT id, name, url, description, source, date_found
   274→        FROM startups
   275→        WHERE {} ORDER BY date_found DESC
   276→    '''.format(where_clause)
   277→
   278→    args = list(params)
   279→    if limit is not None:
   280→        query += " LIMIT ?"
   281→        args.append(limit)
   282→    if offset is not None:
   283→        if limit is None:
   284→            query += " LIMIT -1"
   285→        query += " OFFSET ?"
   286→        args.append(offset)
   287→
   288→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   289→        rows = conn.execute(query, args).fetchall()
   290→    results = [_row_to_dict(row) for row in rows]
   291→    logger.debug(
   292→        "db.get_startups_by_sources",
   293→        extra={
   294→            "event": "db.get_startups_by_sources",
   295→            "where": where_clause,
   296→            "limit": limit,
   297→            "offset": offset,
   298→            "returned": len(results),
   299→        },
   300→    )
   301→    return results
   302→
   303→
   304→def count_startups_by_sources(where_clause: str, params: Iterable) -> int:
   305→    """Count startups matching a dynamic WHERE clause."""
   306→    query = 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM startups WHERE {}'.format(where_clause)
   307→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   308→        (count,) = conn.execute(query, params).fetchone()
   309→    logger.debug(
   310→        "db.count_startups_by_sources",
   311→        extra={
   312→            "event": "db.count_startups_by_sources",
   313→            "where": where_clause,
   314→            "count": count,
   315→        },
   316→    )
   317→    return count
   318→
   319→
   320→def get_startups_by_source_key(source_key: str, limit: Optional[int] = None, offset: Optional[int] = None) -> list[Dict[str, Any]]:
   321→    """Fetch startups for a named source key (github, hackernews, producthunt) with pagination."""
   322→    entry = SOURCE_REGISTRY.get(source_key)
   323→    if entry:
   324→        results = get_startups_by_sources(entry["where"], entry["params"], limit, offset)
   325→    else:
   326→        results = get_all_startups(limit, offset)
   327→    logger.debug(
   328→        "db.get_startups_by_source_key",
   329→        extra={
   330→            "event": "db.get_startups_by_source_key",
   331→            "source_key": source_key,
   332→            "limit": limit,
   333→            "offset": offset,
   334→            "returned": len(results),
   335→        },
   336→    )
   337→    return results
   338→
   339→
   340→def count_startups_by_source_key(source_key: str) -> int:
   341→    """Count startups for a named source key."""
   342→    entry = SOURCE_REGISTRY.get(source_key)
   343→    if entry:
   344→        count = count_startups_by_sources(entry["where"], entry["params"])
   345→    else:
   346→        count = count_all_startups()
   347→    logger.debug(
   348→        "db.count_startups_by_source_key",
   349→        extra={
   350→            "event": "db.count_startups_by_source_key",
   351→            "source_key": source_key,
   352→            "count": count,
   353→        },
   354→    )
   355→    return count
   356→
   357→
   358→def get_source_counts() -> Dict[str, int]:
   359→    """Aggregate startup counts grouped by source category."""
   360→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   361→        rows = conn.execute("SELECT source, COUNT(*) as count FROM startups GROUP BY source").fetchall()
   362→
   363→    summary: Dict[str, int] = {"total": 0, "other": 0}
   364→    for key in SOURCE_REGISTRY:
   365→        summary[key] = 0
   366→
   367→    for row in rows:
   368→        source = row["source"]
   369→        count = row["count"]
   370→        summary["total"] += count
   371→        bucket = classify_source(source)
   372→        summary[bucket] += count
   373→    logger.debug(
   374→        "db.get_source_counts",
   375→        extra={"event": "db.get_source_counts", "summary": summary},
   376→    )
   377→    return summary
   378→
   379→
   380→def get_all_startups(limit: Optional[int] = None, offset: Optional[int] = None) -> list[Dict[str, Any]]:
   381→    """Fetch all startups ordered by date, with optional pagination."""
   382→    query = '''
   383→        SELECT id, name, url, description, source, date_found
   384→        FROM startups ORDER BY date_found DESC
   385→    '''
   386→    params: list = []
   387→    if limit is not None:
   388→        query += " LIMIT ?"
   389→        params.append(limit)
   390→    if offset is not None:
   391→        if limit is None:
   392→            query += " LIMIT -1"
   393→        query += " OFFSET ?"
   394→        params.append(offset)
   395→
   396→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   397→        rows = conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
   398→
   399→    results = [_row_to_dict(row) for row in rows]
   400→    logger.debug(
   401→        "db.get_all_startups",
   402→        extra={
   403→            "event": "db.get_all_startups",
   404→            "limit": limit,
   405→            "offset": offset,
   406→            "returned": len(results),
   407→        },
   408→    )
   409→    return results
   410→
   411→
   412→def get_existing_startup_keys() -> list[Dict[str, str]]:
   413→    """Return existing startup name/url pairs for fast duplicate pre-filtering."""
   414→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   415→        rows = conn.execute('SELECT name, url FROM startups').fetchall()
   416→
   417→    results = [{"name": row["name"], "url": row["url"]} for row in rows]
   418→    logger.debug(
   419→        "db.get_existing_startup_keys",
   420→        extra={"event": "db.get_existing_startup_keys", "returned": len(results)},
   421→    )
   422→    return results
   423→
   424→
   425→def count_all_startups() -> int:
   426→    """Return the total number of startups in the database."""
   427→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   428→        (count,) = conn.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM startups').fetchone()
   429→    logger.debug(
   430→        "db.count_all_startups",
   431→        extra={"event": "db.count_all_startups", "count": count},
   432→    )
   433→    return count
   434→
   435→
   436→def search_startups(query: str, limit: int = 20, offset: int = 0) -> list[Dict[str, Any]]:
   437→    """Search startups using FTS5 full-text search."""
   438→    if not query:
   439→        return []
   440→
   441→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   442→        rows = conn.execute(
   443→            '''
   444→            SELECT s.id, s.name, s.url, s.description, s.source, s.date_found
   445→            FROM startups s
   446→            JOIN startups_fts fts ON s.id = fts.rowid
   447→            WHERE startups_fts MATCH ?
   448→            ORDER BY rank
   449→            LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
   450→            ''',
   451→            (query, limit, offset),
   452→        ).fetchall()
   453→
   454→    results = [_row_to_dict(row) for row in rows]
   455→    logger.debug(
   456→        "db.search_startups",
   457→        extra={
   458→            "event": "db.search_startups",
   459→            "query": query,
   460→            "limit": limit,
   461→            "offset": offset,
   462→            "returned": len(results),
   463→        },
   464→    )
   465→    return results
   466→
   467→
   468→def count_search_results(query: str) -> int:
   469→    """Count FTS matches for the given query string."""
   470→    if not query:
   471→        return 0
   472→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   473→        (count,) = conn.execute(
   474→            '''
   475→            SELECT COUNT(*) FROM startups_fts
   476→            WHERE startups_fts MATCH ?
   477→            ''',
   478→            (query,),
   479→        ).fetchone()
   480→    logger.debug(
   481→        "db.count_search_results",
   482→        extra={"event": "db.count_search_results", "query": query, "count": count},
   483→    )
   484→    return count
   485→
   486→
   487→def get_startup_by_url(url: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   488→    """Fetch a single startup by its URL."""
   489→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   490→        row = conn.execute('''
   491→            SELECT id, name, url, description, source, date_found
   492→            FROM startups WHERE url = ?
   493→        ''', (url,)).fetchone()
   494→
   495→    result = _row_to_dict(row) if row else None
   496→    logger.debug(
   497→        "db.get_startup_by_url",
   498→        extra={"event": "db.get_startup_by_url", "url": url, "found": bool(result)},
   499→    )
   500→    return result
   501→
   502→
   503→def record_scrape_completion(scrapers_run: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
   504→    """Record that a scrape has completed, replacing any previous entry."""
   505→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   506→        c = conn.cursor()
   507→        # Clear old entries and insert new one
   508→        c.execute('DELETE FROM scrape_log')
   509→        c.execute('''
   510→            INSERT INTO scrape_log (last_scrape, scrapers_run)
   511→            VALUES (?, ?)
   512→        ''', (datetime.now().isoformat(), scrapers_run))
   513→        conn.commit()
   514→    logger.info(
   515→        "db.scrape_log_updated",
   516→        extra={
   517→            "event": "db.scrape_log_updated",
   518→            "scrapers_run": scrapers_run,
   519→        },
   520→    )
   521→
   522→
   523→def get_last_scrape_time() -> Optional[str]:
   524→    """Return the ISO timestamp of the most recent completed scrape."""
   525→    with _db_connection() as conn:
   526→        c = conn.cursor()
   527→        c.execute('SELECT last_scrape FROM scrape_log ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1')
   528→        row = c.fetchone()
   529→
   530→    result = row[0] if row else None
   531→    logger.debug(
   532→        "db.get_last_scrape_time",
   533→        extra={"event": "db.get_last_scrape_time", "last_scrape": result},
   534→    )
   535→    return result
   536→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""Master scraper that runs all data sources."""
     3→
     4→import importlib.util
     5→import uuid
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→
     8→from dotenv import load_dotenv
     9→
    10→BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    11→load_dotenv(BASE_DIR / ".env")
    12→
    13→from database import init_db, record_scrape_completion
    14→from logging_config import get_logger, logging_context
    15→
    16→logger = get_logger("devtools.scraper.runner")
    17→
    18→SCRAPER_ENTRYPOINTS: dict[str, list[str]] = {
    19→    "scrape_github_trending": ["scrape_github_trending"],
    20→    "scrape_hackernews": ["scrape_hackernews", "scrape_hackernews_show"],
    21→    "scrape_producthunt_api": ["scrape_producthunt_api"],
    22→}
    23→
    24→
    25→def run_scraper(module_name: str, description: str) -> bool:
    26→    """Run a scraper module and handle any errors."""
    27→    run_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    28→    with logging_context(scraper_runner_module=module_name, scrape_run_id=run_id):
    29→        logger.info(
    30→            "runner.start_scraper",
    31→            extra={"event": "runner.start_scraper", "description": description},
    32→        )
    33→        try:
    34→            spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(module_name, f"{module_name}.py")
    35→            module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    36→            spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    37→
    38→            fn_names = SCRAPER_ENTRYPOINTS.get(module_name)
    39→            if fn_names:
    40→                for fn_name in fn_names:
    41→                    getattr(module, fn_name)()
    42→            else:
    43→                logger.warning(
    44→                    "runner.missing_entrypoint",
    45→                    extra={"event": "runner.missing_entrypoint"},
    46→                )
    47→        except Exception:
    48→            # Intentionally broad: scraper modules may raise arbitrary exceptions
    49→            logger.exception(
    50→                "runner.scraper_failed",
    51→                extra={"event": "runner.scraper_failed"},
    52→            )
    53→            return False
    54→        logger.info(
    55→            "runner.scraper_complete",
    56→            extra={"event": "runner.scraper_complete"},
    57→        )
    58→        return True
    59→
    60→def main() -> None:
    61→    """Run all scrapers."""
    62→    logger.info("runner.start", extra={"event": "runner.start"})
    63→
    64→    # Initialize database
    65→    init_db()
    66→
    67→    # Define scrapers to run
    68→    scrapers = [
    69→        ("scrape_github_trending", "GitHub Trending Repositories"),
    70→        ("scrape_hackernews", "Hacker News & Show HN"),
    71→        ("scrape_producthunt_api", "Product Hunt API"),
    72→    ]
    73→
    74→    successful_names = []
    75→    total_scrapers = len(scrapers)
    76→
    77→    for module_name, description in scrapers:
    78→        if run_scraper(module_name, description):
    79→            successful_names.append(description)
    80→
    81→    logger.info(
    82→        "runner.summary",
    83→        extra={
    84→            "event": "runner.summary",
    85→            "successful_scrapers": len(successful_names),
    86→            "total_scrapers": total_scrapers,
    87→        },
    88→    )
    89→
    90→    # Record the scrape completion
    91→    record_scrape_completion(', '.join(successful_names))
    92→
    93→    if successful_names:
    94→        logger.info(
    95→            "runner.success_notice",
    96→            extra={"event": "runner.success_notice"},
    97→        )
    98→
    99→if __name__ == "__main__":
   100→    main()
   101→

1→"""OpenAI-powered classifier for identifying developer tools from scraped content."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→import os
     5→import threading
     6→from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
     7→from pathlib import Path
     8→from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional
     9→
    10→from cachetools import TTLCache
    11→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    12→import openai
    13→from tenacity import Retrying, retry_if_exception, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential
    14→
    15→# Load environment variables from .env file before importing modules that depend on them
    16→BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    17→load_dotenv(BASE_DIR / ".env")
    18→
    19→from logging_config import get_logger, logging_context
    20→from observability import trace_external_call
    21→
    22→# Initialize LLM Observability before creating OpenAI client
    23→from ddtrace.llmobs import LLMObs
    24→
    25→def _strtobool(val: Optional[str]) -> bool:
    26→    """Convert a string environment variable to a boolean."""
    27→    return str(val).lower() not in {"0", "false", "none", "", "null"}
    28→
    29→_llmobs_enabled = _strtobool(os.getenv("DD_LLMOBS_ENABLED", "1"))
    30→_llmobs_ml_app = (
    31→    os.getenv("DD_LLMOBS_ML_APP")
    32→    or os.getenv("LLMOBS_ML_APP")
    33→    or os.getenv("DD_SERVICE")
    34→    or "devtoolscrape"
    35→)
    36→
    37→if _llmobs_enabled:
    38→    LLMObs.enable(
    39→        ml_app=_llmobs_ml_app,
    40→        api_key=os.getenv("DATADOG_API_KEY"),
    41→        site=os.getenv("DD_SITE", "datadoghq.com"),
    42→        agentless_enabled=False,
    43→        env=os.getenv("DD_ENV"),
    44→        service=os.getenv("DD_SERVICE"),
    45→    )
    46→
    47→# Set up OpenAI client
    48→client = openai.OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY'))
    49→_OPENAI_MODEL = os.getenv("OPENAI_MODEL", "gpt-3.5-turbo")
    50→
    51→logger = get_logger("devtools.ai")
    52→
    53→# Feature flags and tuning knobs
    54→_CACHE_ENABLED = os.getenv("AI_CLASSIFIER_DISABLE_CACHE", "0") != "1"
    55→_CACHE_TTL = float(os.getenv("AI_CLASSIFIER_CACHE_TTL", "3600"))
    56→_CACHE_SIZE = int(os.getenv("AI_CLASSIFIER_CACHE_SIZE", "2048"))
    57→_USE_BATCH = os.getenv("AI_CLASSIFIER_DISABLE_BATCH", "0") != "1"
    58→_BATCH_SIZE = max(1, int(os.getenv("AI_CLASSIFIER_BATCH_SIZE", "8")))
    59→_MAX_CONCURRENCY = max(1, int(os.getenv("AI_CLASSIFIER_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "4")))
    60→_MAX_RETRIES = max(1, int(os.getenv("AI_CLASSIFIER_MAX_RETRIES", "3")))
    61→
    62→
    63→def _has_openai_key() -> bool:
    64→    """Return True when an OpenAI API key is configured."""
    65→    return bool(os.getenv('OPENAI_API_KEY'))
    66→
    67→
    68→_classification_cache = TTLCache(_CACHE_SIZE, _CACHE_TTL)
    69→_category_cache = TTLCache(_CACHE_SIZE, _CACHE_TTL)
    70→_cache_lock = threading.RLock()
    71→
    72→
    73→def _cache_get(cache: TTLCache, key: str) -> Optional[Any]:
    74→    """Look up a cached value, returning None on miss or when caching is disabled."""
    75→    if not _CACHE_ENABLED:
    76→        return None
    77→    with _cache_lock:
    78→        return cache.get(key)
    79→
    80→
    81→def _cache_set(cache: TTLCache, key: str, value: Any) -> None:
    82→    """Store a value in the cache unless caching is disabled."""
    83→    if not _CACHE_ENABLED:
    84→        return
    85→    with _cache_lock:
    86→        cache[key] = value
    87→
    88→DEVTOOLS_KEYWORDS = [
    89→    "developer", "devtool", "CLI", "SDK", "API", "code", "coding", "debug", "git",
    90→    "CI", "CD", "DevOps", "terminal", "IDE", "framework", "testing", "monitoring",
    91→    "observability", "build", "deploy", "infra", "cloud-native", "backend", "log",
    92→    "linter", "formatter", "package manager", "dependency", "compiler", "interpreter",
    93→    "container", "kubernetes", "docker", "microservice", "serverless", "database",
    94→    "query", "schema", "migration", "deployment", "orchestration", "automation",
    95→]
    96→
    97→_DEVTOOLS_KEYWORDS_LOWER = [kw.lower() for kw in DEVTOOLS_KEYWORDS]
    98→
    99→
   100→def has_devtools_keywords(text: str, name: str = "") -> bool:
   101→    """Quick keyword pre-filter to avoid unnecessary API calls."""
   102→    combined_text = f"{name} {text}".lower()
   103→    return any(keyword in combined_text for keyword in _DEVTOOLS_KEYWORDS_LOWER)
   104→
   105→def _cache_key(name: str, text: str) -> str:
   106→    """Build a normalised cache key from a tool name and description."""
   107→    return f"{name.strip().lower()}|{text.strip().lower()}"
   108→
   109→
   110→def _is_retryable_error(exc: Exception) -> bool:
   111→    """Return True for transient OpenAI errors worth retrying."""
   112→    if exc is None:
   113→        return False
   114→    message = str(exc).lower()
   115→    return any(keyword in message for keyword in ("rate limit", "timeout", "429"))
   116→
   117→
   118→def _build_openai_retry() -> Retrying:
   119→    """Create a tenacity Retrying instance for OpenAI API calls."""
   120→    return Retrying(
   121→        stop=stop_after_attempt(_MAX_RETRIES),
   122→        wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=1),
   123→        retry=retry_if_exception(_is_retryable_error),
   124→        reraise=True,
   125→    )
   126→
   127→
   128→def _call_openai(messages: List[Dict[str, str]], max_tokens: int, temperature: float = 0.0, response_format: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None) -> Any:
   129→    """Send a chat completion request to OpenAI with automatic retries."""
   130→    retrying = _build_openai_retry()
   131→    for attempt in retrying:
   132→        with attempt:
   133→            attempt_number = attempt.retry_state.attempt_number
   134→            logger.debug(
   135→                "openai.request",
   136→                extra={
   137→                    "event": "openai.request",
   138→                    "attempt": attempt_number,
   139→                    "max_tokens": max_tokens,
   140→                    "temperature": temperature,
   141→                    "response_format": bool(response_format),
   142→                },
   143→            )
   144→            try:
   145→                with trace_external_call(
   146→                    "openai.chat.completion",
   147→                    resource="classifier.batch",
   148→                    span_type="llm",
   149→                    tags={
   150→                        "openai.model": _OPENAI_MODEL,
   151→                        "openai.attempt": attempt_number,
   152→                        "openai.max_tokens": max_tokens,
   153→                        "openai.temperature": temperature,
   154→                        "openai.response_format": bool(response_format),
   155→                        "openai.message_count": len(messages),
   156→                    },
   157→                ) as span:
   158→                    if span:
   159→                        span.set_tag("span.kind", "client")
   160→                        span.set_tag("component", "openai")
   161→                    response = client.chat.completions.create(
   162→                        model=_OPENAI_MODEL,
   163→                        messages=messages,
   164→                        max_tokens=max_tokens,
   165→                        temperature=temperature,
   166→                        response_format=response_format,
   167→                    )
   168→                    if span and getattr(response, "usage", None):
   169→                        usage = response.usage
   170→                        span.set_tag("openai.prompt_tokens", getattr(usage, "prompt_tokens", 0))
   171→                        span.set_tag("openai.completion_tokens", getattr(usage, "completion_tokens", 0))
   172→                        span.set_tag("openai.total_tokens", getattr(usage, "total_tokens", 0))
   173→                    return response
   174→            except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - relies on API behaviour
   175→                # Intentionally broad: log any API error and let tenacity decide whether to retry
   176→                logger.warning(
   177→                    "openai.error",
   178→                    extra={
   179→                        "event": "openai.error",
   180→                        "attempt": attempt_number,
   181→                        "error": str(exc),
   182→                    },
   183→                )
   184→                raise
   185→
   186→
   187→def classify_candidates(candidates: Iterable[Dict[str, str]]) -> Dict[str, bool]:
   188→    """
   189→    Batch classify multiple candidates. Each candidate must contain keys:
   190→    - id: unique identifier
   191→    - name: tool name
   192→    - text: description
   193→    """
   194→    candidates = list(candidates)
   195→    results: Dict[str, bool] = {}
   196→    pending: List[Dict[str, str]] = []
   197→
   198→    for candidate in candidates:
   199→        cid = candidate["id"]
   200→        name = candidate.get("name", "")
   201→        text = candidate.get("text", "")
   202→        with logging_context(candidate_id=cid, candidate_name=name):
   203→            if not has_devtools_keywords(text, name):
   204→                logger.debug(
   205→                    "classifier.keyword_filter",
   206→                    extra={
   207→                        "event": "classifier.keyword_filter",
   208→                    },
   209→                )
   210→                results[cid] = False
   211→                continue
   212→            key = _cache_key(name, text)
   213→            cached = _cache_get(_classification_cache, key)
   214→            if cached is not None:
   215→                logger.debug(
   216→                    "classifier.cache_hit",
   217→                    extra={
   218→                        "event": "classifier.cache_hit",
   219→                        "outcome": cached,
   220→                    },
   221→                )
   222→                results[cid] = cached
   223→            else:
   224→                candidate["_cache_key"] = key
   225→                pending.append(candidate)
   226→
   227→    if not pending:
   228→        return results
   229→
   230→    if not _has_openai_key() or not _USE_BATCH or len(pending) == 1:
   231→        for candidate in pending:
   232→            outcome = _classify_single(candidate.get("name", ""), candidate.get("text", ""))
   233→            _cache_set(_classification_cache, candidate["_cache_key"], outcome)
   234→            results[candidate["id"]] = outcome
   235→        return results
   236→
   237→    chunks = [pending[i:i + _BATCH_SIZE] for i in range(0, len(pending), _BATCH_SIZE)]
   238→
   239→    def worker(chunk):
   240→        payload = [
   241→            {
   242→                "item_id": candidate["id"],
   243→                "name": candidate.get("name", ""),
   244→                "description": candidate.get("text", ""),
   245→            }
   246→            for candidate in chunk
   247→        ]
   248→        try:
   249→            response = _call_openai(
   250→                [
   251→                    {
   252→                        "role": "system",
   253→                        "content": (
   254→                            "Classify each item as devtools-related. "
   255→                            "Respond with JSON object {\"results\": {\"<item_id>\": \"yes\"|\"no\", ...}}. "
   256→                            "If unsure, respond with \"no\"."
   257→                        ),
   258→                    },
   259→                    {"role": "user", "content": json.dumps(payload)},
   260→                ],
   261→                max_tokens=len(payload) * 20 + 50,
   262→                temperature=0.0,
   263→                response_format={"type": "json_object"},
   264→            )
   265→            content = response.choices[0].message.content.strip()
   266→            data = json.loads(content)
   267→            result_map = data.get("results", {})
   268→        except Exception as exc:
   269→            # Intentionally broad: any batch failure falls back to per-item classification
   270→            logger.exception(
   271→                "classifier.batch_failure",
   272→                extra={"event": "classifier.batch_failure", "error": str(exc)},
   273→            )
   274→            result_map = {}
   275→
   276→        if not result_map:
   277→            for candidate in chunk:
   278→                with logging_context(candidate_id=candidate["id"], candidate_name=candidate.get("name")):
   279→                    outcome = _classify_single(candidate.get("name", ""), candidate.get("text", ""))
   280→                    _cache_set(_classification_cache, candidate["_cache_key"], outcome)
   281→                    results[candidate["id"]] = outcome
   282→        else:
   283→            for candidate in chunk:
   284→                with logging_context(candidate_id=candidate["id"], candidate_name=candidate.get("name")):
   285→                    answer = result_map.get(candidate["id"])
   286→                    if answer is None:
   287→                        logger.warning(
   288→                            "classifier.batch_missing_id",
   289→                            extra={
   290→                                "event": "classifier.batch_missing_id",
   291→                                "candidate_id": candidate["id"],
   292→                            },
   293→                        )
   294→                        outcome = _classify_single(candidate.get("name", ""), candidate.get("text", ""))
   295→                    else:
   296→                        outcome = str(answer).strip().lower() == "yes"
   297→                    logger.debug(
   298→                        "classifier.batch_result",
   299→                        extra={
   300→                            "event": "classifier.batch_result",
   301→                            "outcome": outcome,
   302→                        },
   303→                    )
   304→                    _cache_set(_classification_cache, candidate["_cache_key"], outcome)
   305→                    results[candidate["id"]] = outcome
   306→
   307→    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(_MAX_CONCURRENCY, len(chunks))) as executor:
   308→        futures = [executor.submit(worker, chunk) for chunk in chunks]
   309→        for future in as_completed(futures):
   310→            future.result()
   311→
   312→    return results
   313→
   314→
   315→def _classify_single(name: str, text: str) -> bool:
   316→    """Classify a single candidate via the OpenAI API, falling back to keywords."""
   317→    if not has_devtools_keywords(text, name):
   318→        return False
   319→    if not _has_openai_key():
   320→        logger.warning(
   321→            "classifier.no_api_key",
   322→            extra={"event": "classifier.no_api_key"},
   323→        )
   324→        return is_devtools_related_fallback(text)
   325→
   326→    prompt = f"""
   327→    You are a classifier that determines if software/tools are developer tools (devtools).
   328→
   329→    Devtools include:
   330→    - Development tools (IDEs, text editors, debuggers)
   331→    - Build tools, package managers, CI/CD tools
   332→    - Testing frameworks, monitoring tools
   333→    - API tools, SDKs, libraries
   334→    - DevOps tools, infrastructure tools
   335→    - Code analysis, linting, formatting tools
   336→    - Database tools, deployment tools
   337→    - Terminal tools, CLI applications
   338→    - Developer productivity tools
   339→
   340→    NOT devtools:
   341→    - End-user applications (games, social media, productivity apps)
   342→    - Business software, marketing tools
   343→    - Consumer apps, entertainment apps
   344→    - E-commerce, finance apps (unless specifically for developers)
   345→
   346→    Content to classify:
   347→    Name: {name}
   348→    Description: {text}
   349→
   350→    Answer with ONLY "yes" or "no".
   351→    """
   352→    try:
   353→        response = _call_openai(
   354→            [
   355→                {
   356→                    "role": "system",
   357→                    "content": (
   358→                        "You are a binary classifier for developer tools. "
   359→                        "Respond with EXACTLY 'yes' or 'no'. If uncertain, respond 'no'."
   360→                    ),
   361→                },
   362→                {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
   363→            ],
   364→            max_tokens=5,
   365→            temperature=0.0,
   366→        )
   367→        answer = response.choices[0].message.content.strip().lower()
   368→        if answer not in {"yes", "no"}:
   369→            logger.warning(
   370→                "classifier.unexpected_answer",
   371→                extra={
   372→                    "event": "classifier.unexpected_answer",
   373→                    "answer": answer,
   374→                    "tool_name": name,
   375→                },
   376→            )
   377→            return is_devtools_related_fallback(text)
   378→        return answer == "yes"
   379→    except Exception as exc:
   380→        # Intentionally broad: any classification failure degrades to keyword matching
   381→        logger.exception(
   382→            "classifier.single_error",
   383→            extra={"event": "classifier.single_error", "tool_name": name},
   384→        )
   385→        return is_devtools_related_fallback(text)
   386→
   387→
   388→def is_devtools_related_ai(text: str, name: str = "") -> bool:
   389→    """
   390→    Use OpenAI to classify if content is devtools-related.
   391→    Returns True if it's devtools, False otherwise.
   392→    """
   393→    key = _cache_key(name, text)
   394→    cached = _cache_get(_classification_cache, key)
   395→    if cached is not None:
   396→        return cached
   397→    result = classify_candidates([{"id": "_single", "name": name, "text": text}]).get("_single", False)
   398→    _cache_set(_classification_cache, key, result)
   399→    return result
   400→
   401→def is_devtools_related_fallback(text: str) -> bool:
   402→    """Fallback keyword-based classifier when AI is unavailable."""
   403→    text_lower = text.lower()
   404→    return any(keyword in text_lower for keyword in _DEVTOOLS_KEYWORDS_LOWER)
   405→
   406→
   407→def get_devtools_category(text: str, name: str = "") -> Optional[str]:
   408→    """
   409→    Get a more specific category for the devtool.
   410→    Returns category like 'IDE', 'CLI Tool', 'Testing', etc.
   411→    """
   412→    if not _has_openai_key():
   413→        return None
   414→
   415→    key = _cache_key(name, text)
   416→    cached = _cache_get(_category_cache, key)
   417→    if cached is not None:
   418→        return cached
   419→    
   420→    prompt = f"""
   421→    Classify this devtool into one of these categories:
   422→    - IDE/Editor: Integrated development environments, code editors
   423→    - CLI Tool: Command line tools, terminal applications
   424→    - Testing: Testing frameworks, test runners, mocking tools
   425→    - Build/Deploy: Build tools, deployment tools, CI/CD
   426→    - Monitoring/Observability: Logging, metrics, tracing, alerting
   427→    - Database: Database tools, ORMs, query builders
   428→    - API/SDK: API tools, SDKs, client libraries
   429→    - DevOps: Infrastructure, containerization, orchestration
   430→    - Code Quality: Linters, formatters, static analysis
   431→    - Package Manager: Dependency management, package managers
   432→    - Other: Anything else
   433→
   434→    Examples:
   435→    Name: VSCode
   436→    Description: A code editor for developers.
   437→    Category: IDE/Editor
   438→
   439→    Name: GitHub Actions
   440→    Description: A CI/CD automation tool for code repositories.
   441→    Category: Build/Deploy
   442→
   443→    Name: Postman
   444→    Description: API development and testing tool.
   445→    Category: API/SDK
   446→
   447→    Name: Datadog
   448→    Description: Cloud monitoring and observability platform.
   449→    Category: Monitoring/Observability
   450→
   451→    Name: Slack
   452→    Description: A team chat and collaboration app.
   453→    Category: Other
   454→
   455→    Name: QuickBooks
   456→    Description: Accounting software for small businesses.
   457→    Category: Other
   458→
   459→    Name: {name}
   460→    Description: {text}
   461→
   462→    Respond with ONLY the category name.
   463→    """
   464→
   465→    try:
   466→        response = _call_openai(
   467→            [
   468→                {
   469→                    "role": "system",
   470→                    "content": (
   471→                        "You are a devtool categorizer. Respond with EXACTLY one of the specified category names. "
   472→                        "If the tool doesn't fit, respond with 'Other'."
   473→                    ),
   474→                },
   475→                {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
   476→            ],
   477→            max_tokens=15,
   478→            temperature=0.0,
   479→        )
   480→
   481→        category = response.choices[0].message.content.strip()
   482→        _cache_set(_category_cache, key, category)
   483→        logger.debug(
   484→            "classifier.category",
   485→            extra={
   486→                "event": "classifier.category",
   487→                "tool_name": name,
   488→                "category": category,
   489→            },
   490→        )
   491→        return category
   492→
   493→    except Exception as e:
   494→        # Intentionally broad: category failures return None rather than crashing
   495→        logger.exception(
   496→            "classifier.category_error",
   497→            extra={"event": "classifier.category_error", "tool_name": name},
   498→        )
   499→        return None
   500→

content.content:
1→"""
     2→Lightweight helpers for Datadog tracing so we can consistently instrument
     3→outbound calls even when ddtrace isn't available (e.g., in tests).
     4→"""
     5→from __future__ import annotations
     6→
     7→import os
     8→from contextlib import contextmanager
     9→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Dict, Iterator, Optional
    10→
    11→if TYPE_CHECKING:  # pragma: no cover - typing aid only
    12→    from ddtrace.span import Span  # type: ignore
    13→
    14→try:  # pragma: no cover - ddtrace may not be installed in all environments
    15→    from ddtrace import tracer  # type: ignore
    16→except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - fallback to no-op tracing
    17→    tracer = None  # type: ignore
    18→
    19→DEFAULT_SERVICE = os.getenv("DD_SERVICE", "devtoolscrape")
    20→
    21→
    22→@contextmanager
    23→def trace_http_call(
    24→    resource: str,
    25→    method: str,
    26→    url: str,
    27→    *,
    28→    service: Optional[str] = None,
    29→    span_name: str = "external.http.request",
    30→) -> Iterator[Optional["Span"]]:
    31→    """
    32→    Wrap an outbound HTTP call in a Datadog span if ddtrace is available.
    33→
    34→    Returns the span so callers can attach additional tags (status code, etc.).
    35→    When ddtrace isn't installed the context manager yields ``None``.
    36→    """
    37→    if tracer is None:
    38→        yield None
    39→        return
    40→
    41→    effective_service = service or DEFAULT_SERVICE
    42→
    43→    with tracer.trace(resource, service=effective_service, resource=url, span_type="http") as span:
    44→        span.set_tag("http.method", method.upper())
    45→        span.set_tag("http.url", url)
    46→        yield span
    47→
    48→
    49→@contextmanager
    50→def trace_external_call(
    51→    span_name: str,
    52→    resource: str,
    53→    *,
    54→    service: Optional[str] = None,
    55→    span_type: str = "custom",
    56→    tags: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    57→) -> Iterator[Optional["Span"]]:
    58→    """
    59→    Generic tracing helper for non-HTTP calls (e.g., OpenAI SDK).
    60→    """
    61→    if tracer is None:
    62→        yield None
    63→        return
    64→
    65→    effective_service = service or DEFAULT_SERVICE
    66→
    67→    with tracer.trace(span_name, service=effective_service, resource=resource, span_type=span_type) as span:
    68→        if tags:
    69→            for key, value in tags.items():
    70→                span.set_tag(key, value)
    71→        yield span
    72→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→"""Structured JSON logging with Datadog trace correlation."""
     2→
     3→import logging
     4→import logging.config
     5→import logging.handlers
     6→import os
     7→import socket
     8→import sys
     9→from contextlib import contextmanager
    10→from contextvars import ContextVar
    11→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    12→from typing import Any, Dict, Iterator, Optional
    13→
    14→try:  # pragma: no cover - optional dependency
    15→    from pythonjsonlogger import jsonlogger
    16→except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - fail fast if missing
    17→    raise RuntimeError("python-json-logger is required for structured logging") from exc
    18→
    19→try:  # pragma: no cover - optional ddtrace correlation
    20→    from ddtrace.helpers import get_correlation_ids  # type: ignore
    21→
    22→    _DDTRACE_AVAILABLE = True
    23→except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
    24→    _DDTRACE_AVAILABLE = False
    25→
    26→
    27→_CONTEXT: ContextVar[Dict[str, Any]] = ContextVar("devtools_log_context", default={})
    28→_CONFIGURED = False
    29→
    30→
    31→def _iso_timestamp() -> str:
    32→    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(timespec="milliseconds")
    33→
    34→
    35→def _resolve_log_dir() -> str:
    36→    requested = os.getenv("LOG_DIR", "/var/log/devtoolscrape")
    37→    fallback = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "logs")
    38→    for path in (requested, fallback):
    39→        try:
    40→            os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True)
    41→            return path
    42→        except OSError:
    43→            continue
    44→    # As a last resort, do not configure file logging
    45→    print("Warning: unable to create log directory; defaulting to stdout only", file=sys.stderr)
    46→    return ""
    47→
    48→
    49→class DatadogJSONFormatter(jsonlogger.JsonFormatter):
    50→    """Custom JSON formatter that injects ISO timestamps and standard keys."""
    51→
    52→    def add_fields(self, log_record: Dict[str, Any], record: logging.LogRecord, message_dict: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    53→        super().add_fields(log_record, record, message_dict)
    54→
    55→        if "timestamp" not in log_record:
    56→            log_record["timestamp"] = getattr(record, "timestamp", _iso_timestamp())
    57→        if "level" not in log_record:
    58→            log_record["level"] = record.levelname
    59→        if "logger" not in log_record:
    60→            log_record["logger"] = record.name
    61→        if "message" not in log_record:
    62→            log_record["message"] = record.getMessage()
    63→
    64→        # Ensure consistent casing for Datadog attributes
    65→        if hasattr(record, "dd_trace_id") and record.dd_trace_id:
    66→            log_record["dd.trace_id"] = record.dd_trace_id
    67→        if hasattr(record, "dd_span_id") and record.dd_span_id:
    68→            log_record["dd.span_id"] = record.dd_span_id
    69→
    70→        # Preserve numeric types when possible
    71→        for key, value in list(log_record.items()):
    72→            if isinstance(value, datetime):
    73→                log_record[key] = value.isoformat()
    74→
    75→
    76→class ContextFilter(logging.Filter):
    77→    """Populate log records with shared fields from context vars and defaults."""
    78→
    79→    def __init__(self, service: str, env: str, hostname: str) -> None:
    80→        super().__init__()
    81→        self.service = service
    82→        self.env = env
    83→        self.hostname = hostname
    84→
    85→    def filter(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> bool:  # pragma: no cover - simple data attachment
    86→        record.timestamp = _iso_timestamp()
    87→        record.service = getattr(record, "service", self.service)
    88→        record.env = getattr(record, "env", self.env)
    89→        record.hostname = getattr(record, "hostname", self.hostname)
    90→
    91→        context = _CONTEXT.get()
    92→        for key, value in context.items():
    93→            setattr(record, key, value)
    94→
    95→        if _DDTRACE_AVAILABLE:
    96→            try:
    97→                trace_id, span_id = get_correlation_ids()
    98→            except Exception:
    99→                trace_id = span_id = None
   100→            if trace_id:
   101→                record.dd_trace_id = str(trace_id)
   102→            if span_id:
   103→                record.dd_span_id = str(span_id)
   104→        return True
   105→
   106→
   107→def setup_logging() -> None:
   108→    """Configure base logging for the application."""
   109→    global _CONFIGURED
   110→    if _CONFIGURED:
   111→        return
   112→
   113→    service = os.getenv("DD_SERVICE", "devtoolscrape")
   114→    env = os.getenv("DD_ENV", os.getenv("LOG_ENV", "local"))
   115→    hostname = socket.gethostname()
   116→    log_level = os.getenv("LOG_LEVEL", "INFO").upper()
   117→    log_format = os.getenv("LOG_FORMAT", "json").lower()
   118→    log_dir = _resolve_log_dir()
   119→    log_filename = os.getenv("LOG_FILENAME", "app.log")
   120→    log_path = os.path.join(log_dir, log_filename) if log_dir else None
   121→    log_to_stdout = os.getenv("LOG_STDOUT", "true").lower() in {"1", "true", "yes"}
   122→
   123→    formatters = {
   124→        "json": {
   125→            "()": "logging_config.DatadogJSONFormatter",
   126→            "fmt": "%(timestamp)s %(level)s %(message)s",
   127→        },
   128→        "console": {
   129→            "format": "%(timestamp)s | %(levelname)s | %(name)s | %(message)s",
   130→        },
   131→    }
   132→
   133→    filters = {
   134→        "context": {
   135→            "()": "logging_config.ContextFilter",
   136→            "service": service,
   137→            "env": env,
   138→            "hostname": hostname,
   139→        }
   140→    }
   141→
   142→    handlers: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
   143→
   144→    if log_path:
   145→        handlers["file"] = {
   146→            "class": "logging.handlers.RotatingFileHandler",
   147→            "formatter": "json" if log_format == "json" else "console",
   148→            "filters": ["context"],
   149→            "filename": log_path,
   150→            "maxBytes": int(os.getenv("LOG_MAX_BYTES", 10 * 1024 * 1024)),
   151→            "backupCount": int(os.getenv("LOG_BACKUP_COUNT", "5")),
   152→            "encoding": "utf-8",
   153→        }
   154→
   155→    if log_to_stdout or not handlers:
   156→        handlers["stdout"] = {
   157→            "class": "logging.StreamHandler",
   158→            "formatter": "json" if log_format == "json" else "console",
   159→            "filters": ["context"],
   160→            "stream": "ext://sys.stdout",
   161→        }
   162→
   163→    config = {
   164→        "version": 1,
   165→        "disable_existing_loggers": False,
   166→        "formatters": formatters,
   167→        "filters": filters,
   168→        "handlers": handlers,
   169→        "root": {
   170→            "level": log_level,
   171→            "handlers": list(handlers.keys()),
   172→        },
   173→        "loggers": {
   174→            "devtools": {
   175→                "level": log_level,
   176→                "propagate": False,
   177→                "handlers": list(handlers.keys()),
   178→            },
   179→            "openai": {
   180→                "level": os.getenv("OPENAI_LOG_LEVEL", "WARNING"),
   181→            },
   182→            "urllib3": {
   183→                "level": os.getenv("HTTP_LOG_LEVEL", "WARNING"),
   184→            },
   185→        },
   186→    }
   187→
   188→    logging.config.dictConfig(config)
   189→    _CONFIGURED = True
   190→
   191→
   192→def get_logger(name: str) -> logging.Logger:
   193→    setup_logging()
   194→    return logging.getLogger(name)
   195→
   196→
   197→def bind_context(**kwargs: Any) -> None:
   198→    current = dict(_CONTEXT.get())
   199→    for key, value in kwargs.items():
   200→        if value is None:
   201→            continue
   202→        current[key] = value
   203→    _CONTEXT.set(current)
   204→
   205→
   206→def unbind_context(*keys: str) -> None:
   207→    if not keys:
   208→        _CONTEXT.set({})
   209→        return
   210→    current = dict(_CONTEXT.get())
   211→    for key in keys:
   212→        current.pop(key, None)
   213→    _CONTEXT.set(current)
   214→
   215→
   216→@contextmanager
   217→def logging_context(**kwargs: Any) -> Iterator[None]:
   218→    previous: Dict[str, Any] = dict(_CONTEXT.get())
   219→    bind_context(**kwargs)
   220→    try:
   221→        yield
   222→    finally:
   223→        _CONTEXT.set(previous)
   224→
